purpose: Estimate the quality and commercial-cultural seriousness of a visitor session using event-based intent signals rather than vanity metrics.

base_rules.reason: Core signal of directed interest in a CREARE experience.
Editorial discovery signal that often precedes deeper experience interest.
Curiosity and research signal, but weaker than direct inquiry behavior.
Clear expression of explicit private-interest intent.
Strong progression from curiosity into inquiry action.
Highest-confidence live intent outcome in the current model.

session_modifiers.reason: Boost sessions that GA4 recognizes as meaningfully engaged.
Repeat attention often indicates stronger human intent than one-off browsing.

dashboard_use_cases: Identify high-value sessions before inquiry completion.
Rank experiences by quality of attention, not just view volume.
Separate editorial discovery from commercially meaningful intent.
Build executive weekly reporting around qualified human demand.
Support Looker Studio scorecards for High Intent Sessions and Qualified Human Visitors.
limitations: This model is heuristic and should not be treated as a deterministic lead score.
It depends on internal and developer traffic filters being verified.
Returning-session logic requires reliable GA4 availability or downstream modeling.
Outbound clicks can reflect distraction as well as interest.
Supplier or partner traffic is not yet distinctly classified.
The model is strongest when read at session or cohort level, not person-level identity.
